channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Uncertainty Surrounds U.S.-Iran Talks as Cease-Fire Nears End
item.description: Iran and the US are uncertain about talks as the ceasefire nears its end. Iran War Live Updates: Uncertainty Surrounds U. S.-Iran Talks as Cease-Fire Nears End The New York Times.

item.title: US-Iran talks are in doubt as ceasefire nears an end
item.description: US-Iran talks are in doubt as the ceasefire deadline approaches. Live updates: US-Iran talks are in doubt as ceasefire nears an end AP News.

item.title: Cuba confirms recent talks with visiting U.S. delegation
item.description: Cuba confirmed recent talks with a visiting US delegation. The U. S. negotiators laid out several conditions for negotiations with Cuba to continue, including the release of prominent political prisoners. Cuba confirms recent talks with visiting U. S.

item.title: Uncertainty swirls around US-Iran peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms
item.description: Uncertainty surrounds US-Iran peace talks as the ceasefire deadline looms. Live updates: Uncertainty swirls around US-Iran peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms CNN.

item.title: Trump suggests he won’t end ceasefire with Iran even if talks are productive
item.description: Trump said he may not end the Iran ceasefire even if talks are productive. Iran war live updates: Trump suggests he won’t end ceasefire with Iran even if talks are productive MS NOW.

item.title: US "Ready To Go" On War Path. Iran Prepares Fresh Targets, "Surprises"
item.description: Iran prepares for potential war with the US, citing "new surprises" if necessary. The country is ready to respond to emerging situations, with fresh targets being considered. Iran has also said that it is prepared to go to war with "new surprises" if the possibility arises.

item.title: Fired U.K. Official Describes ‘Pressure’ to Expedite Envoy’s Appointment
item.description: A fired UK official claims Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office pressured them to quickly appoint Peter Mandelson, an associate of Jeffrey Epstein, as ambassador to Washington. Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s office wanted Peter Mandelson, a Jeffrey Epstein associate, swiftly installed as ambassador to Washington, the official said.

item.title: EU court rules Hungary’s LGBTQ law violates human rights
item.description: The European Court of Justice ruled that Hungary's LGBTQ law, introduced by PM Viktor Orban's government, breaches EU human rights law on multiple levels. European Court of Justice says measure by PM Viktor Orban's government breaches EU law 'on a number of separate levels'.

item.title: Uncertainty clouds Iran talks as ceasefire deadline nears
item.description: Uncertainty surrounds Iran's participation in talks as a ceasefire deadline approaches. Pakistan's Information Minister stated that a formal response from Iran is still awaited. As of 7:30 p. m. Pakistan time, a formal response about Iran’s participation “is still awaited”, Pakistan’s Information Minister said in a statement.

item.title: Can Israel’s economy sustain conflicts on multiple fronts?
item.description: Israel's economy faces challenges due to ongoing conflicts, with the war with Iran costing 35 billion shekels ($11.52 billion) and operations in Gaza expected to cost around 8.6% of its annual GDP by 2025.

item.title: Moment hikers get caught in Guatemala volcano eruption
item.description: Israeli soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention for vandalizing a Jesus statue in Lebanon. The punishment was announced by Israel's military. A group of hikers were forced to flee as Guatemala’s Santiaguito Volcano erupted.

item.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
item.description: Guatemala's Santiaguito Volcano erupted, forcing a group of hikers to flee. The volcano's eruption is a rare occurrence. Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

item.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
item.description: Iran's war with the US and Israel has led to mass redundancies in the country's manufacturing, retail, and digital sectors. The impact is expected to worsen if the war resumes.

item.title: 'Unprovoked Acts Of Terror': UAE Minister To NDTV On Iran's Drone Attacks
item.description: The UAE described Iran's drone attacks as "unprovoked terrorist attacks." The statement was made by UAE Minister Saeed Bin Mubarak Al Hajeri. Saeed Bin Mubarak Al Hajeri described the strikes as "unprovoked terrorist attacks".

item.title: Yet to receive formal response on Iranian participation in talks: Pakistan Information Minister
item.description: Pakistan's Information Minister said the country has yet to receive a formal response on Iranian participation in talks. Meanwhile, over 11.6 lakh Indians have returned from West Asia amid the conflict.

item.title: Iran Foreign Ministry condemns arrests in UAE as 'baseless'
item.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry condemned the UAE's arrest of Iranian nationals as "baseless". Iran has accused Gulf states of allowing US forces to carry out attacks from their territory, and has launched missile and drone strikes against US interests in the region.

item.title: Trump urges Iran to release ‘8 women facing execution’ ahead of possible peace talks
item.description: Donald Trump urged Iran to release eight women facing execution, ahead of possible peace talks. He suggested their release could boost chances of success in negotiations between the US and Iran.

item.title: Trump says U.S. in ‘very, very strong’ position for Iran talks
item.description: Trump said the US is in a "very, very strong" position for Iran talks. He also suggested Iran could increase the chances of successful peace talks by freeing the eight women who face execution.

item.title: US forces board sanctioned oil tanker in Indian Ocean as Iran ceasefire deadline looms
item.description: US forces boarded a sanctioned oil tanker in the Indian Ocean. The move comes as a deadline for Iran to cease hostilities looms, raising tensions in the region. US forces board sanctioned oil tanker in Indian Ocean as Iran ceasefire deadline looms.

item.title: Nikki Haley claims China-aided Iran cargo ship seized by US. Then a Beijing ‘hype’ response
item.description: Nikki Haley claimed the US had seized a China-aided cargo ship carrying Iranian goods. China responded, calling Haley's claims "hype", a statement that highlights the diplomatic tensions between the US, China, and Iran.
